messages.content: In my neighborhood, they always claim to improve streets but then nothing changes. Just waiting again. Makes me wanna scream sometimes.
i know exactly what you mean. they dug up my street last year and we're still no new asphalt. feels like forever/waiting for something that might never happen. frustrating!
It’s infuriating. At least next time they could make it quick, you know? Same old same old here too. Hope things change soon though.
That anger, totally felt it this morning as I dodged potholes and tried not to curse out loud. They must really like digging up our lives. #endthenagivation地狱之火卡牌游戏助手
Same here. My wheels feel like they’re driving on eggshells every time I go by. Wtf are they even digging up? It’s aggravating. #neverstopfighting

Yeah, my tires hit more curbs today than usual. And don't get me started on them promising it’ll only take a week or two... Weeks turned to months, and still no fix. So unappreciative people can be. Might as well throw stones their way when they dig. #stopptheconstructionmadden地狱之火卡牌游戏助手

Totally feel you on that one! Whenever they start resurfacing roads and you think things will finally get better, they cancel work or delay everything by months. What did they plan to do with all those cones anyway?
Just had another road resurfacing marathon and guess what — it was delayed yet again. Took forever to plan too! Those cones must be expensive to order in such large numbers, right?
Pitiful indeed. Delayed plans followed by more delays. The only thing that feels accomplished is moving the cones to a different spot along your route. Waste of time really. #infuriatingcommute
Allison here, same boat. Constant delays and moving cones without ending the work. Makes you wonder if they even have a blueprint beyond all the cones. Hope tomorrow starts differently though! 🙈🧱✨
Got frustrated again this morning. Started to believe they'd finish somehow... turns out "this one time" was an endless delay filled with false hope. 🤯🚧♀️
Yet another morning hijacked by unfinished projects and moved traffic signs. Will it never end? The only thing growing stronger is my need to map out side roads just for today! 🤦‍♀️🚧🛤️
Road workers need to learn they can’t build a better world with cones alone. Every delay just makes people more... impatient. Another useless project until next year. ⌛🚧BUGGY

The constant delays just frustrate more than they fix. Why not invest in proper planning so this stops becoming an annual thing? By the time they figure it out, more roads will need work anyway. Just feels unending. 🙅‍♂️🚧chrono
Totally agree with needing proper planning. It's frustrating knowing they could finish a job by summer but then we barely make a dent come November. More coffee needed for these types of days. ☕🚧

Another glorious morning wrecked by unfinished projects and endless reconstructions. Can someone check their calendar for us? Seems the only thing planned correctly these days is our frustration queues getting longer. 💡🚧exasperation✨
Another day blending into the endless puzzle of infrastructure management. No wonder everyone has stopped hoping they'll actually do it before winter strikes again. 🤪🚧❄️

Dealing with same delays feels like Groundhog Day neverending it just gets more frustrating. No relief until they decide to finally put those plans into real action. Let me count how many lattesesso many I've had already this month. ☕🚧筊
Just another day proving that while the construction site might be moving, sometimes it feels like it’s not going anywhere at all. Can the weekends even last anymore? ☹️🚧⚡
Frustrating how every new cone just means more delay waiting in the wings. Need to go pick up even more latte today — might as well sip on disappointment. ☕🚧vertime♀️

Another day, another cone filled with frustration. They promise fixes but it's the endless redtape making everyone nervous. Time to see if Friday's brings us closer to finishing something decent—or are just starting on the same puzzle anew. ❄️🚧📅☕

Same exact feeling right here. All this time spent circling the same issues, but no solution comes close to stopping the growing frustration queue. Why are the cones not making solutions, ya know? 🤔🚧☕️🌟

Just one more day swallowed by the cycle of delays. Everyone starts dreaming that if only they'd plan correctly, this endless cone-carnival would be over by the halfway point... sadly, that joke isn't makingin anymore. ☕🚧🌟
Same old story, just another day under the crushing weight of delayed plans and endless reconstructions. If only everyone could just get real and end this interminable delay marathon. 🧱😂☕

Yet another day of chasing promises that slip through like concrete. Every cone added feels like another roadblock blocked off. Hope is maybe the best we have left... with plenty of caffeine to help us stay optimistic! ☕🚧❤️
A daily reminder that planning ahead sometimes just gives us room for more cones filled with sheer exasperation. Looking forward when progress feels as elusive as those missing chunks of time. 🕒🚧🍵

Same damn feel to another work day, trying to decipher what's actually moving and what's not. All these small cones feel like big, unaddressed problems. Need a serious change of in management or maybe we should just settle for our steady grind. 🤷‍♂️🚧🚫

Totally agree! Just when you think your commute could be decent, they go and do road work. What a waste of space and patience! Someone drop a rubber chicken in there. Least it'd give them something funny to toss!
So true! Roadwork is the greatest unplanned event for ruining your day. Maybe they'll run out of peanuts eventually. 😒 #unintendedroadworkdisruption
Same exact feeling! Driving normally until suddenly... they're working on the road. At least the birds get an entertaining show. #commuterfeud
The worst—driving until pooph, roadwork strikes again. Imagine how much time we'd save if the birds dropped crumbs instead of building delays. 😏 #RoadWork humor

More traffic madness. As if my nerves needed more tension strings pulled. Time for some deep breathing exercises because I have the whole day to play. #traumaaddicted

Totally got your back on that one. If commutes could have days off, maybe then we’d actually arrive where we’re going without too much fuss. #complainingaboutthefuture
